plan: Step 1: Create terrain vertex + edge shaders — `src/visual/shaders/terrain.vert.glsl` and `src/visual/shaders/terrain.frag.glsl` for LineSegments edges, plus `src/visual/shaders/terrainVertex.vert.glsl` and `src/visual/shaders/terrainVertex.frag.glsl` for Points vertices. Vertex shader must: (a) accept uniforms uTime, uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uOpacity, uMotionAmplitude, uPaletteHue, uPaletteSaturation, uCadence, uNoiseFrequency, uNoiseOctaves, uPointerPos, uPointerDisturbance; (b) use noise3d.glsl FBM to compute bass-driven macro Y-displacement (rolling wave across X/Z using uTime + uBassEnergy * amplitude); (c) add treble-driven per-vertex jitter (high-freq noise scaled by uTrebleEnergy); (d) pass vColor computed from palette hue + height-based brightness. Fragment shader: apply chromaticDispersion.glsl, output vColor * uOpacity. Points vertex shader additionally sets gl_PointSize with treble shimmer modulation.

Step 2: Create the terrain heightfield system — `src/visual/systems/terrainHeightfield.ts`. Implement GeometrySystem interface following wireframePolyhedra.ts pattern. Config interface: `TerrainHeightfieldConfig { rows?: number; cols?: number; noiseOctaves?: 1|2|3 }`. In `init()`: (a) call `generateTerrainHeightfield()` from US-073 generator with seed, rows, cols; (b) extract unique vertices using `extractUniqueVertices()` from edge positions; (c) create LineSegments mesh with BufferGeometry from generator positions + randoms (aRandom attribute); (d) create Points mesh from unique vertex positions with aRandom attribute; (e) create two ShaderMaterials (edges + vertices) with all required uniforms; (f) position camera: elevated angle looking toward horizon (e.g. camera override or scene-level framing — set meshes' position/rotation so terrain appears as a landscape receding into depth). In `draw()`: update all uniforms from frame.params (bassEnergy, trebleEnergy, time, pointer, palette, cadence, etc). Implement `cleanup()` to dispose geometry + material + remove from scene. Implement `setOpacity()` to update uOpacity uniform on both materials.

Step 3: Set up camera framing for terrain perspective — In the system's `init()`, position the terrain group so the perspective camera sees depth toward a horizon. Options: (a) rotate the terrain mesh group slightly (tilt ~20-30° around X so it recedes); (b) offset Y position downward so camera looks across the surface; (c) use a THREE.Group to hold both meshes and apply transforms to the group. The existing camera is at (0,0,5) with FOV 60 — the terrain (default 10×10 units) should be positioned/scaled to fill the view with visible depth/parallax. Consider: group.position.set(0, -2, -3) and group.rotation.x = -Math.PI * 0.3.

Step 4: Register terrain mode in main.ts — (a) Import `createTerrainHeightfield` from the new system file; (b) create instance with quality-driven config: `createTerrainHeightfield({ rows: quality.terrainRows, cols: quality.terrainCols, noiseOctaves: quality.noiseOctaves })`; (c) add to singleEntries array: `{ kind: 'single', name: 'terrain', system: terrain, maxPoints: (quality.terrainRows + 1) * (quality.terrainCols + 1) }`; (d) add to systemRegistry for compound modes: `terrain: (cfg) => createTerrainHeightfield(cfg as ...)`. Quality tiers already define terrainRows/terrainCols (low: 8×8, high tiers scale up). The `extractSystemConfig('terrain', ...)` path already exists in quality.ts.

Step 5: Verify quality tier scaling — Confirm quality.ts tier values for terrainRows/terrainCols are reasonable. Low=8×8 seems very sparse for a recognizable terrain; may need to bump: low→20×30, medium→40×60, high→60×90 (matching the values documented in the agent exploration). Check what the current tier definitions actually set and adjust if the existing values are placeholder minimums from the safety floor. Quality tiers must reduce grid density (not canvas size) per acceptance criteria.

Step 6: Validate shader integrity and baseline mode — (a) Build the project (`npm run build`) and verify no shader compile errors; (b) test in browser with ?debug to confirm no WebGL validation errors; (c) verify terrain renders a stable first frame; (d) test baseline mode: when bassEnergy=0 and trebleEnergy=0, terrain must still render with its static FBM shape (no modulation = no displacement beyond base heightfield); (e) verify mute toggle and info overlay still work; (f) confirm no console errors on normal load; (g) verify no forbidden storage APIs used.

Step 7: Test audio reactivity — (a) Verify bass energy drives visible macro vertical displacement waves rolling across terrain; (b) verify treble drives fine vertex jitter and point size shimmer on vertices; (c) confirm audio reactivity continues when muted (analyser still polls); (d) confirm scene works when autoplay is blocked (terrain renders with static shape).
testing_recommendations: Build gate: `npm run build` must pass with zero errors
Shader integrity: load with ?debug, confirm no WebGL compile/validation errors in console
Baseline rendering: set audio to muted, verify terrain renders static FBM shape without modulation — no blank/broken scene
Bass reactivity: play audio, verify macro Y-displacement waves visibly roll across terrain grid proportional to bass energy
Treble reactivity: verify vertex points shimmer (size oscillation) and fine jitter appears during high-treble passages
Autoplay blocked: block autoplay in browser settings, reload — terrain must render and remain visible
Mode rotation: let ModeManager cycle through modes — verify terrain appears in rotation, transitions smoothly (opacity fade), and cleans up without WebGL resource leaks
Quality tier scaling: test with ?debug on simulated low-tier device — confirm grid density reduces (fewer edges/vertices) while canvas remains full-screen
Camera framing: verify perspective depth — terrain should recede toward a horizon with visible parallax, not appear as a flat orthographic grid
Memory/performance: monitor GPU memory and frame rate during terrain mode — no per-frame object allocation, buffer updates only
risks: Shader compile errors from GLSL typos or undeclared symbols — mitigate by keeping shaders minimal and testing incrementally
Camera framing may not look good with the shared camera at (0,0,5) — terrain needs careful positioning/rotation to show depth; may need to adjust mesh group transform iteratively
Quality tier values for terrain (low=8×8) may be too sparse to be recognizable as terrain — need to verify and potentially increase minimum grid density
Bass displacement amplitude tuning — too high makes terrain chaotic, too low is imperceptible; needs manual tuning against actual audio tracks
LineSegments + Points dual-mesh approach doubles draw calls — acceptable for terrain but should verify no frame drops on low-tier devices

The generator produces edge-pair positions (6 floats per edge) which is already formatted for LineSegments, but extracting unique vertices for Points requires deduplication — must use extractUniqueVertices correctly to avoid duplicate points
